title: HUD Environmental Review Officer Designation and Property Disposition Updates
summary: Kimone Jordan (NBD Director of Housing) presents item 30: designating Deputy Commissioner Dishaw as environmental review certifying officer per new HUD guidance that preparers cannot sign off on their own reviews. NBD staff will prepare NEPA/SEQR reviews; Dishaw will certify. Brief property updates: 635-637 North McBride Street in final 30-day surplus/foreclosure notice from Law Department; 532 Court Street going to land bank resolution before council.
raw_content:
[00:51:34] >> We have item 30. Also, Jake, um, if you
[00:51:36] can just give I'm cuz I'm confused by
[00:51:41] >> Oh, uh, I'll I'll try to do my best.
[00:51:43] >> Oh, Kimone used to do it. It's going
[00:51:46] from Kimone to me, so I'll let her talk
[00:51:47] about it.
[00:51:47] >> Okay.
[00:51:49] >> All right. Good afternoon, counselors.
[00:51:50] Kimone Jordan, director of housing and
[00:51:52] neighborhood planning. So, this is a
[00:51:54] request to have uh, deputy commissioner
[00:51:56] Desaw named as the environmental review
[00:51:58] certifying officer. So, for every
[00:52:00] project that we put federal funds into,
[00:52:02] we need to complete an environmental
[00:52:04] review. We were informed by HUD a few
[00:52:06] weeks ago that the person preparing the
[00:52:08] review cannot be the person that signs
[00:52:10] off. So, we would have NBD staff like
[00:52:12] myself, um, people in zoning who would
[00:52:15] complete the review and then Jake would
[00:52:16] just sign off as the officer.
[00:52:18] >> So, on CPC, is Mera that person? Does
[00:52:24] Mera do that [clears throat]
[00:52:27] >> in zoning? So, I'm conducting the
[00:52:29] environmental reviews currently.
[00:52:31] >> I'm sorry.
[00:52:34] >> The the officeration
[00:52:37] does the uh seeker reviews. Uh mayor um
[00:52:41] is just the land use attorney from corp
[00:52:43] council that's staffing um the zoning
[00:52:46] office and our boards. Um but the staff
[00:52:49] do the reviews and ultimately um as
[00:52:52] zoning administrator it falls under my
[00:52:54] purview. So I think what Kimon's saying
[00:52:56] is this would just shift um in align
[00:52:59] with that
[00:53:00] >> path to you.
[00:53:01] >> It would shift from her to me.
[00:53:03] >> Oh. From you to her. Okay.
[00:53:04] >> Yes. So this is
[00:53:07] >> because you're the writer.
[00:53:08] >> So I'm the currently I am the preparer.
[00:53:11] So I'm doing the review and I'm signing
[00:53:13] off on them.
[00:53:14] >> Okay.
[00:53:14] >> We I can't do both. So Jake will sign
[00:53:16] off and I will do the reviews. Okay.
[00:53:18] >> And NEPA is separate from Seeker.
[00:53:20] There's two separate environmental
[00:53:21] review processes.
[00:53:22] >> Okay.
[00:53:25] Come on. I have one question. Sure. Uh,
[00:53:27] >> can you give me an update on the 635 637
[00:53:31] East Well, North McBride Street?
[00:53:34] >> Yeah. So, from my understanding, I
[00:53:35] believe the law department was working
[00:53:36] to send out the final 30-day and surplus
[00:53:39] notice to foreclose. I can take a look
[00:53:41] and follow up.
[00:53:42] >> Please do. All right. I appreciate.
[00:53:43] Thank you.
[00:53:44] >> And I know you had another court street
[00:53:45] address that you were curious about.
[00:53:46] >> Yeah. 532 as well.
[00:53:48] >> So, it'll go before the land bank for a
[00:53:50] resolution. After that, we can bring it
[00:53:51] before council.
[00:53:52] >> Okay. Thank you.
[00:53:55] >> Any questions?
[00:53:57] >> Thank you.
[00:53:58] >> You all set?
[00:54:01] >> Yeah.
[00:54:01] >> Yes.
[00:54:02] >> Councelor Monto,

processing_notes: NEPA and SEQR are separate environmental review processes. HUD = US Department of Housing and Urban Development. All federally funded projects require environmental review.
Segment 11 of 14 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 4th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
